title: Nominations Sent to the Senate for United States Attorney for the District of Nevada, Assistant Secretary of State for Educational and Cultural Affairs, and Assistant Secretary of the Interior
summary: The White House announced that nominations have been sent to the Senate for several federal positions. The nominees include George Kelesis to be United States Attorney for the District of Nevada for a four-year term, succeeding Jason M. Frierson; Catherine Dillon to be Assistant Secretary of State for Educational and Cultural Affairs, succeeding Lee Satterfield who resigned; and William Hague to be Assistant Secretary of the Interior. The notice indicates that additional nominations are included in the submission.

title: Strengthening United States National Defense Through an Expanded Clean Coal Power Generation Fleet
summary: This presidential action outlines the objective of ensuring the resilience and reliability of the electric grid that supports military installations, operations, and defense-industrial production. It states that the grid underpins national defense and economic stability and aims to prevent prolonged disruptions caused by energy supply variability by reducing reliance on intermittent energy sources. The measure proposes expanding a fleet of clean coal power generation facilities to bolster grid reliability and support national defense and economic security.
